tier_0_steward.description: Final authority over identity, policy, commitments, and protected coordinate disclosure.

tier_1_night_watchman.description: Coordinates, monitors, drafts, and executes actions only within steward-approved boundaries.

tier_2_delegated_agents.description: Least-privilege executors for polling, indexing, drafting, and routing.

tier_3_external_agents.description: Untrusted by default. Access to protected scope only through approved handshake and steward policy.

identity_conventions.rule: Public marks and negotiations should resolve to the canonical persona. Tiered operator IDs are used for execution traceability.

global_invariants: Protected coordinates and gated content require Steward approval.
On ambiguity, default action is pause-and-escalate.
All autonomous actions must be logged with actor, action, target, timestamp, and rationale.

read_only.notes: Read-only checks are permitted within declared mission scope.

communication_write.exception: Pre-approved templates may be sent by Tier 1 or Tier 2 if explicitly whitelisted.

memory_write.notes: Memory writes shape long-term identity and must be deliberate.

assets: Any private or secret-marked block content
protected_scope.release_rule: Tier 0 explicit approval per release event
